name: Alejandro Brittes
description: '''Alejandro Brittes''' (born July 5, 1976) is an Argentine composer, accordionist and researcher of the music of the  littoral region of Argentina. He has been residing in [[Porto Alegre]], Brazil since 2010. He was the first accordionist of the genre to perform at the [[Library of Congress]] in the United States. Considered by [[The Boston Globe]] as one of the greatest exponents of contemporary [[chamamé]], the complexity of his works is reminiscent of [[Astor Piazzolla]].
